title: Add role-specific evaluation rubrics to agent prompts
source: Research on self-evaluation prompting — agents that know their grading criteria produce 25-35% higher quality output (Constitutional AI / RLHF-inspired self-evaluation patterns)
description: Current agent prompts define what to do but not how quality is measured. Adding explicit evaluation rubrics (what Rick would score them on) creates a self-evaluation loop where agents check their own work against criteria before submitting. This is especially effective for reviewer-morty and security-morty whose output quality directly impacts the entire pipeline. The rubric acts as an implicit 'verify' step that's more specific than the generic reasoning_protocol.
proposed_changes: Add a <self_evaluation> XML section to the prompt template in _build_agent_prompt() (after </uncertainty_policy>, line 505). Each role gets a tailored rubric. Example for backend-morty: '<self_evaluation>Before submitting, verify: (1) All acceptance criteria are met — check each one explicitly, (2) New code has error handling for external calls, (3) Unit tests cover happy path + at least 1 edge case, (4) No hardcoded secrets or config values, (5) Code follows existing patterns in the project. If any check fails, fix it before reporting.</self_evaluation>'. Security-morty gets OWASP-mapped checks. Reviewer-morty gets a code quality checklist. Architect-morty gets interface completeness checks. Store rubric strings in a ROLE_RUBRICS dict alongside AGENT_PROMPTS.
